title: Getting started with typethis
text: lowest-level entry points test, assert, validate types. Use function boundaries, parsing inputs, anywhere quick sanity check enough. is_type() accepts : builtin name — "numeric", "integer", "character", "logical", "list", "data.frame", "matrix", "factor", "date", "posixct", "function", "environment"; predicate function function(value) -> logical; type spec t_union("integer", "character"). safe coercion, use coerce_type():
code: is_type(5, "numeric") #> [1] TRUE is_type("hello", "numeric") #> [1] FALSE  # assert_type() throws on mismatch assert_type(5, "numeric", "x")  # validate_type() returns a list instead of throwing validate_type("hello", "numeric", "x") #> $valid #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $error #> [1] "Type error: 'x' must be numeric, got character" coerce_type("123", "numeric") #> [1] 123 coerce_type(c(1, 0, 1), "logical") #> [1]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE

title: Getting started with typethis
text: typed_function() wraps function call validated. bad call fails body runs: typed_function() supports every R calling convention — positional, named, mixed, reordered, ... passthrough — respects argument defaults. Set coerce = TRUE convert mismatches checking, validate = FALSE hot paths ’ve already validated upstream. full feature set see ?typed_function validators--models vignette.
code: greet <- typed_function(   function(name, times = 1L) paste(rep(name, times), collapse = " "),   arg_specs = list(name = "character", times = "integer"),   return_spec = "character" )  greet("hi", times = 3L) #> [1] "hi hi hi" greet("hi", times = "three") #> Error: #> ! Type error: 'times' must be integer, got character

title: Getting started with typethis
text: want add type checks function already , as_typed() removes boilerplate restating every argument. Specs arguments literal atomic defaults inferred automatically; arguments want override appear call. Pass overrides name. Inference fills rest: Pass name = NULL opt one argument validation, .infer = FALSE disable inference entirely. See ?as_typed ?infer_specs full inference rules. existing typed function, types() symmetric replacement-form accessor as_typed(): retrofit whole environment one call, as_typed_env() walks environment, applies as_typed() every function finds, writes typed versions back. Per-function overrides flow .specs:
code: add <- as_typed(function(x = 0L, y = 0L) x + y, .return = "integer") add(2L, 3L) #> [1] 5 add("two", 3L) #> Error: #> ! Type error: 'x' must be integer, got character greet <- as_typed(   function(name = "world", times = 1L) {     paste(rep(name, times), collapse = " ")   },   name = t_vector_of("character", exact_length = 1L) ) greet("hi", times = 3L) #> [1] "hi hi hi" greet_clone <- function(name = "world", times = 1L) {   paste(rep(name, times), collapse = " ") } types(greet_clone) <- types(greet) is_typed(greet_clone) #> [1] TRUE  # NULL un-types types(greet_clone) <- NULL is_typed(greet_clone) #> [1] FALSE e <- new.env() e$add <- function(x = 0L, y = 0L) x + y e$greet <- function(name = "world") paste0("hi ", name)  as_typed_env(e, .specs = list(   add = list(.return = "integer") )) is_typed(e$add) #> [1] TRUE attr(e$add, "return_spec") #> [1] "integer"

title: Getting started with typethis
text: Use define_model() describe record type — configuration object, API request body, domain entity. generates new_<Class>() constructor update_<Class>() updater calling environment. Validation runs construction time: update_User() returns fresh instance named fields replaced revalidated: Models nest. Reference one model another class name: defaults, nullability, strict mode, validators, see validators--models vignette.
code: define_model("User", fields = list(   name = field("character"),   age = field("integer",     validator = numeric_range(0, 120),     default = 0L   ),   email = field("character",     validator = string_pattern("^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$")   ) ))  ada <- new_User(name = "Ada Lovelace", email = "ada@example.com") ada #> <Typed Model: User> #> Fields: #>   name: character = Ada Lovelace #>   email: character = ada@example.com #>   age: integer = 0 new_User(name = "Ada", age = 200L, email = "ada@example.com") #> Error: #> ! Validation failed for field 'age' in User ada <- update_User(ada, age = 36L) ada$age #> [1] 36 define_model("Address", fields = list(   street = field("character"),   city   = field("character") ))  define_model("Person", fields = list(   name    = field("character"),   address = field("Address") ))  p <- new_Person(   name    = "Ada",   address = new_Address(street = "Newstead Abbey", city = "Nottingham") ) p$address$city #> [1] "Nottingham"

title: Getting started with typethis
text: t_*() family builds richer specifications work everywhere plain type name (is_type(), assert_type(), field(), typed_function(), to_json_schema()). Specs compose: Use spec inside field() typed_function()’s arg_specs entry. See type-specs vignette full reference.
code: id <- t_union("integer", "character") is_type(1L, id) #> [1] TRUE is_type("u-42", id) #> [1] TRUE  maybe_int <- t_nullable("integer") is_type(NULL, maybe_int) #> [1] TRUE  tags <- t_list_of("character", min_length = 1L) is_type(list("alpha", "beta"), tags) #> [1] TRUE  role <- t_enum(c("admin", "user", "guest")) is_type("admin", role) #> [1] TRUE mixed <- t_list_of(t_union("integer", "character")) is_type(list(1L, "two", 3L), mixed) #> [1] TRUE

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: to_json_schema() turns typed models, type specs, validators, [field()] definitions Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema fragment plain R list. Key behaviours: Built-validator constraints (numeric_range, string_length, string_pattern, vector_length, enum_validator) become native JSON Schema keywords (minimum, maxLength, pattern, minItems, enum). Nested models become $ref entries $defs. Cyclic references handled via stub--fill protocol. Constructs without canonical mapping (data frames, factors, custom predicate functions) emit x-typethis-* extension keys round-trip typethis-aware tooling. accepted inputs include type specs (to_json_schema(t_union(...))), validator closures (to_json_schema(numeric_range(0, 10))), field() definitions.
code: define_model("Person", fields = list(   name = field("character", nullable = FALSE),   age  = field("integer", validator = numeric_range(0, 120)),   role = field(t_enum(c("admin", "user")), default = "user") ))  schema <- to_json_schema("Person") str(schema, max.level = 2) #> List of 6 #>  $ $schema             : chr "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema" #>  $ title               : chr "Person" #>  $ type                : chr "object" #>  $ properties          :List of 3 #>   ..$ name:List of 1 #>   ..$ age :List of 3 #>   ..$ role:List of 3 #>  $ additionalProperties: logi TRUE #>  $ required            :List of 2 #>   ..$ : chr "name" #>   ..$ : chr "age" cat(jsonlite::toJSON(schema, auto_unbox = TRUE, pretty = TRUE)) #> { #>   "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema", #>   "title": "Person", #>   "type": "object", #>   "properties": { #>     "name": { #>       "type": "string" #>     }, #>     "age": { #>       "type": "integer", #>       "minimum": 0, #>       "maximum": 120 #>     }, #>     "role": { #>       "type": "string", #>       "enum": [ #>         "admin", #>         "user" #>       ], #>       "default": "user" #>     } #>   }, #>   "additionalProperties": true, #>   "required": [ #>     "name", #>     "age" #>   ] #> }

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: Open Data Contract Standard v3 (ODCS) industry format describing data products. typethis goes ways: typed models export ODCS YAML existing ODCS contracts import back model registry.

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: write_datacontract() file-IO convenience wrapper:
code: define_model("Order", fields = list(   order_id = field("character",     primary_key = TRUE,     validator   = string_pattern("^ORD-[0-9]+$")   ),   amount = field("numeric",     validator = numeric_range(0, 1e6),     pii       = FALSE   ),   status = field(t_enum(c("new", "paid", "shipped")),     default = "new"   ),   customer = field("character",     classification = "confidential",     pii            = TRUE   ) ))  contract <- to_datacontract("Order",   info = list(     name = "orders",     version = "1.0.0",     description = "Order records"   ) )  str(contract, max.level = 2) #> List of 8 #>  $ apiVersion : chr "v3.0.2" #>  $ kind       : chr "DataContract" #>  $ id         : chr "Order" #>  $ status     : chr "draft" #>  $ name       : chr "orders" #>  $ version    : chr "1.0.0" #>  $ description:List of 1 #>   ..$ purpose: chr "Order records" #>  $ schema     :List of 1 #>   ..$ :List of 3 tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".yaml") write_datacontract("Order", tmp,   info = list(name = "orders", version = "1.0.0") ) readLines(tmp, n = 6) #> [1] "apiVersion: v3.0.2" "kind: DataContract" "id: Order"          #> [4] "status: draft"      "name: orders"       "version: 1.0.0"

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: from_datacontract() reads contract back, calls define_model() every entry, assigns generated new_*() update_*() constructors envir.
code: env <- new.env() from_datacontract(tmp, envir = env) ls(env) #> [1] "new_Order"    "update_Order"  env$new_Order(order_id = "ORD-1", amount = 42, customer = "Ada") #> <Typed Model: Order> #> Fields: #>   order_id: character = ORD-1 #>   amount: numeric = 42 #>   customer: character = Ada #>   status: character = new

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: OpenAPI 3.1 JSON-Schema-Draft-2020-12-compatible, JSON Schema machinery powers OpenAPI bridge well. Models become entries components.schemas; $ref strings rewritten #/$defs/X #/components/schemas/X. Typed functions become paths entry whose requestBody carries arguments whose 200 response carries return type.

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
code: define_model("Address2", fields = list(   street = field("character"),   city   = field("character") )) define_model("PersonDoc", fields = list(   id = field("integer",     primary_key = TRUE,     validator = numeric_range(min = 1L)   ),   name = field("character"),   address = field("Address2") ))  doc <- to_openapi(list("PersonDoc", "Address2"),   info = list(title = "People API", version = "1.0.0") ) names(doc$components$schemas) #> [1] "PersonDoc" "Address2"

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
code: greet <- typed_function(   function(name, greeting = "Hi") paste(greeting, name),   arg_specs = list(name = "character", greeting = "character"),   return_spec = "character" ) attr(greet, "openapi_op_id") <- "greet"  doc <- to_openapi(list("PersonDoc", greet),   info = list(title = "People API", version = "1.0.0") ) names(doc$paths) #> [1] "/greet"

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: write_openapi() infers format file extension. Pass format = "json" (use .json path) JSON output.
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".yaml") write_openapi("PersonDoc", tmp,   info = list(title = "People API", version = "1.0.0") )  env <- new.env() from_openapi(tmp, envir = env) ls(env) #> [1] "new_Address2"     "new_PersonDoc"    "update_Address2"  "update_PersonDoc"

code: ns <- new.env() ns$add <- function(x = 0L, y = 0L) x + y ns$normalise <- function(s) tolower(trimws(s))  enable_for_package(ns, .specs = list(   add = list(.return = "integer"),   normalise = list(s = "character", .return = "character") ))  attr(ns$add, "return_spec") #> [1] "integer" attr(ns$normalise, "arg_specs") #> $s #> [1] "character" .onLoad <- function(libname, pkgname) {   typethis::enable_for_package(     pkgname,     .specs = list(       normalise = list(s = "character", .return = "character"),       parse_date = list(x = "character", .return = t_predicate(inherits, "Date"))     )   ) }

title: Type-checking your whole package
text: enable_for_package() skips two categories automatically: Primitives — nothing wrap. Package hooks — .onLoad, .onAttach, .onUnload, .onDetach, .Last.lib, .First.lib. called R’s namespace machinery fixed argument shape wrapped. can narrow .filter (function(name, fn) returning TRUE retrofit, FALSE skip). user filter runs built-skip list, can narrow, never widen:
code: ns2 <- new.env() ns2$public <- function(x = 1L) x ns2$internal_helper <- function(x = 1L) x  enable_for_package(ns2, .filter = function(name, fn) {   !startsWith(name, "internal_") })  is_typed(ns2$public) #> [1] TRUE is_typed(ns2$internal_helper) #> [1] FALSE

title: Type-checking your whole package
text: package loads, every typed binding answers is_typed(): Calling add() wrong type now produces typethis error ’d get hand-written typed_function():
code: ns3 <- new.env() ns3$add <- function(x = 0L, y = 0L) x + y enable_for_package(ns3)  is_typed(ns3$add) #> [1] TRUE get_signature(ns3$add) #> $args #> $args$x #> [1] "integer" #>  #> $args$y #> [1] "integer" #>  #>  #> $return #> NULL #>  #> $formals #> $formals$x #> [1] 0 #>  #> $formals$y #> [1] 0 ns3$add("a", 3L) #> Error: #> ! Type error: 'x' must be integer, got character

what: Lifting types from your existing roxygen docs
title: Type-checking your whole package
text: package already prose documentation, swap enable_for_package() as_typed_from_roxygen(). reads installed .Rd files (roxygen generates), extracts type spec every documented @param @return, forwards enable_for_package(). Specs derived docs cover every documented argument, including without literal defaults — gap inference fill. Two extraction layers run sequence: Explicit [type] tag. @param description begins bracketed text read verbatim: Anything brackets becomes spec string — useful prose ambiguous type one typethis’s compound spec constructors (e.g. [t_vector_of("integer", min_length = 1)]). Vocabulary heuristic. Otherwise leading prose matched [default_type_vocabulary()]. Patterns anchored start description, “numeric vector” matches “Returns numeric value …” . Demonstration synthetic Rd directory: Inference still runs alongside, undocumented arguments literal defaults still get specs infer_specs(). User .specs win , just like enable_for_package(). extend prose vocabulary — e.g. recognise tbl_df data.frame — prepend entry:
code: .onLoad <- function(libname, pkgname) {   typethis::as_typed_from_roxygen(pkgname) } #' @param x [integer] Number of iterations rd_dir <- tempfile("rd") dir.create(rd_dir) writeLines(c(   "\\name{add}",   "\\alias{add}",   "\\title{Add two numbers}",   "\\arguments{",   "\\item{x}{A numeric vector.}",   "\\item{y}{[integer] Number of times.}",   "}",   "\\value{A numeric vector.}" ), file.path(rd_dir, "add.Rd"))  ns4 <- new.env() ns4$add <- function(x, y) rep(x, y)  as_typed_from_roxygen(ns4, .rd_dir = rd_dir)  attr(ns4$add, "arg_specs") #> $x #> [1] "numeric" #>  #> $y #> [1] "integer" attr(ns4$add, "return_spec") #> [1] "numeric" my_vocab <- c(   "(?i)^\\s*(?:a|an)?\\s*tbl[._]df\\b" = "data.frame",   default_type_vocabulary() ) as_typed_from_roxygen("mypkg", .vocabulary = my_vocab)

what: Type-checking a package you do not own
title: Type-checking your whole package
text: two patterns edit target package — fine , awkward dependency. enable_typed_namespace() takes third route: register setHook(packageEvent(..., "onLoad")) handler outside target, next library() call wraps package’s bindings without ever touching source. hook fires R locks namespace, retrofit goes unlock-modify-relock dance (as_typed_env(.unlock = TRUE)). Re-locking guaranteed .exit even wrap step errors, namespace end half-unlocked. package already loaded call enable_typed_namespace(), retrofit also runs immediately live namespace. stop retrofitting package future loads — revert typed wrappers currently loaded copy — call disable_typed_namespace(): Foreign hooks packageEvent left intact: hooks tagged typethis removed. pattern meant development, debugging, exploratory work. ship code calls enable_typed_namespace() another package’s namespace — modifying namespaces developer convenience, CRAN-acceptable production pattern. production use, use enable_for_package() inside .onLoad.
code: typethis::enable_typed_namespace("dplyr") library(dplyr)   # bindings are typed once loaded typethis::disable_typed_namespace("dplyr")

title: Type-checking your whole package
text: Coverage scales much ’ve used defaults. enable_for_package() alone, functions written without literal defaults (e.g. function(x, y) ...) get typed wrapper specs unless fill via .specs. as_typed_from_roxygen() plugs gap lifting types existing prose. Heuristics imperfect. vocabulary recognises common type language; ambiguous prose silently produces spec. Use [type] prefix .specs overrides anything care precisely. Per-call overhead. Every call now passes wrapper validates arguments. hot paths, set .validate = FALSE per call via validate_call() pass .validate = FALSE enable_for_package() / as_typed_from_roxygen(). CRAN. Modifying namespace .onLoad() fine. Modifying another package’s namespace outside separate, invasive pattern functions deliberately support.

title: Type-checking your whole package
text: [as_typed()][as_typed] — retrofit one function time. [as_typed_env()][as_typed_env] — engine enable_for_package() built ; gives full control filtering overrides. [as_typed_from_roxygen()][as_typed_from_roxygen] — lift types prose @param/@return documentation. [parse_param_type()][parse_param_type] — inspect derived single description string. [enable_typed_namespace()][enable_typed_namespace] — retrofit package via setHook(packageEvent(...)). [disable_typed_namespace()][disable_typed_namespace] — revert unregister. [typed_function()][typed_function] — write typed function scratch instead retrofitting.

title: Composable type specifications
text: Use is_type_spec() detect composite spec runtime.

title: Composable type specifications
code: id <- t_union("integer", "character") is_type(1L, id) #> [1] TRUE is_type("u-1", id) #> [1] TRUE is_type(1.5, id) #> [1] FALSE

title: Composable type specifications
code: maybe_int <- t_nullable("integer") is_type(NULL, maybe_int) #> [1] TRUE is_type(1L, maybe_int) #> [1] TRUE is_type("hi", maybe_int) #> [1] FALSE

title: Composable type specifications
text: t_list_of() accepts element type spec, including composites:
code: tags <- t_list_of("character", min_length = 1L) is_type(list("alpha", "beta"), tags) #> [1] TRUE is_type(list(), tags) #> [1] FALSE  triple <- t_vector_of("integer", exact_length = 3L) is_type(1:3, triple) #> [1] TRUE is_type(1:5, triple) #> [1] FALSE mixed <- t_list_of(t_union("integer", "character")) is_type(list(1L, "two", 3L), mixed) #> [1] TRUE

title: Composable type specifications
text: t_enum() type-spec sibling enum_validator(). Use type-spec form want enum appear true type field() typed_function() surface JSON Schema typed enum keyword.
code: role <- t_enum(c("admin", "user", "guest")) is_type("admin", role) #> [1] TRUE is_type("root", role) #> [1] FALSE

title: Composable type specifications
text: References resolve validation time, makes forward references (cycles) possible:
code: define_model("Address", fields = list(zip = field("character"))) addr <- t_model("Address") is_type(new_Address(zip = "10115"), addr) #> [1] TRUE

title: Composable type specifications
text: t_predicate() thin wrapper around custom predicate carries description. description surfaces error messages JSON Schema output.
code: positive <- t_predicate(   function(x) is.numeric(x) && all(x > 0),   description = "positive number" ) is_type(5, positive) #> [1] TRUE is_type(-1, positive) #> [1] FALSE

what: Using specs in models and functions
title: Composable type specifications
text: Anywhere type = arg_specs = accepts builtin name, also accepts spec.
code: define_model("Order", fields = list(   id     = field(t_union("integer", "character")),   status = field(t_enum(c("new", "paid", "shipped")), default = "new"),   tags   = field(t_list_of("character"), default = list()) ))  ord <- new_Order(id = "ord-1", tags = list("priority", "rush")) ord$status #> [1] "new" ord$tags #> [[1]] #> [1] "priority" #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "rush" greet <- typed_function(   function(name, n = 1L) paste(rep(name, n), collapse = " "),   arg_specs = list(     name = "character",     n    = t_union("integer", "numeric")   ),   return_spec = "character" ) greet("hi", n = 3) #> [1] "hi hi hi"

title: Composable type specifications
text: coerce_type() understands subset specs coercion clear meaning: t_nullable(...) — NULL passes ; otherwise inner spec drives coercion; t_union(...) — alternative tried order; first coerces validates wins; t_enum(...) — values already allowed set pass ; otherwise value coerced enum’s value type re-checked. kinds (t_list_of(), t_vector_of(), t_model(), t_predicate()) raise explicit error.
code: coerce_type("paid", t_enum(c("new", "paid", "shipped"))) #> [1] "paid" coerce_type(NULL, t_nullable("integer")) #> NULL coerce_type("123", t_union("integer", "character")) #> [1] 123

title: Validators and models
text: validator just function function(value) -> logical. package ships factories common rules; results carry constraint attribute bridges (JSON Schema, ODCS, OpenAPI) can emit native keywords (minimum, maxLength, pattern, …) instead opaque predicate stubs.

title: Validators and models
code: age <- numeric_range(0, 120) age(25) #> [1] TRUE age(150) #> [1] FALSE  # Probability strictly in (0, 1) prob <- numeric_range(0, 1, exclusive_min = TRUE, exclusive_max = TRUE) prob(0.5) #> [1] TRUE prob(0) #> [1] FALSE

code: name <- string_length(min_length = 1, max_length = 50) name("Ada Lovelace") #> [1] TRUE name("") #> [1] FALSE  email <- string_pattern("^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$") email("user@example.com") #> [1] TRUE email("not-an-email") #> [1] FALSE

title: Validators and models
code: pair <- vector_length(exact_len = 2) pair(c(1, 2)) #> [1] TRUE pair(c(1, 2, 3)) #> [1] FALSE  nums <- list_of("numeric", min_length = 1) nums(list(1, 2, 3)) #> [1] TRUE nums(list("a")) #> [1] FALSE  # Optional values: wrap any validator with `nullable()` optional_num <- nullable(function(x) is.numeric(x)) optional_num(NULL) #> [1] TRUE optional_num(5) #> [1] TRUE

title: Validators and models
code: positive_num <- combine_validators(   function(x) is.numeric(x),   function(x) all(x > 0) ) positive_num(5) #> [1] TRUE positive_num(-1) #> [1] FALSE  num_or_str <- combine_validators(   function(x) is.numeric(x),   function(x) is.character(x),   all_of = FALSE ) num_or_str(5) #> [1] TRUE num_or_str("hi") #> [1] TRUE num_or_str(TRUE) #> [1] FALSE

title: Validators and models
text: validator_constraint() exposes structured constraint attached built-factories — useful tooling bridge functions:
code: validator_constraint(numeric_range(0, 10)) #> $kind #> [1] "numeric_range" #>  #> $min #> [1] 0 #>  #> $max #> [1] 10 #>  #> $exclusive_min #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $exclusive_max #> [1] FALSE validator_constraint(function(x) x > 0) # NULL for plain user predicates #> NULL

title: Validators and models
text: Use define_model() describe record. creates new_<Class>() constructor update_<Class>() helper calling environment.
code: define_model("Person", fields = list(   name = field("character"),   age = field("integer", validator = numeric_range(0, 120)),   email = field("character",     validator = string_pattern("^[^@]+@[^@]+$")   ) ))  p <- new_Person(name = "Ada", age = 36L, email = "ada@example.com") p #> <Typed Model: Person> #> Fields: #>   name: character = Ada #>   age: integer = 36 #>   email: character = ada@example.com

code: define_model("Config", fields = list(   host  = field("character", default = "localhost"),   port  = field("integer", default = 8080L),   debug = field("logical", default = FALSE) ))  new_Config()$host #> [1] "localhost" new_Config(host = "api.example.com", port = 443L)$port #> [1] 443

title: Validators and models
text: field nullable = TRUE allowed NULL (omitted construction without default). Without , omission error unless default set.
code: define_model("Profile", fields = list(   name = field("character"),   bio  = field("character", nullable = TRUE) ))  new_Profile(name = "Ada")$bio #> NULL new_Profile(name = "Ada", bio = "Mathematician")$bio #> [1] "Mathematician"

title: Validators and models
text: .strict = TRUE rejects unknown fields construction:
code: define_model("StrictPoint", fields = list(   x = field("numeric"),   y = field("numeric") ), .strict = TRUE)  new_StrictPoint(x = 1, y = 2, z = 3) #> Error: #> ! Extra fields not allowed for StrictPoint: z

title: Validators and models
text: field’s type may registered model class name. constructor check value instance class. Use t_model("Address") want type spec form can combined t_nullable() t_list_of().
code: define_model("Address", fields = list(   street = field("character"),   city   = field("character") ))  define_model("Person2", fields = list(   name    = field("character"),   address = field("Address") ))  new_Person2(   name    = "Ada",   address = new_Address(street = "Newstead", city = "Nottingham") ) #> <Typed Model: Person2> #> Fields: #>   name: character = Ada #>   address: Address = <Address of length 2>

title: Validators and models
text: field() accepts validator callback runs type check:
code: define_model("Coupon", fields = list(   code = field("character",     validator = string_length(min_length = 4, max_length = 12)   ),   discount = field("numeric",     validator = numeric_range(0, 1, exclusive_max = TRUE)   ) ))  new_Coupon(code = "SAVE20", discount = 0.2) #> <Typed Model: Coupon> #> Fields: #>   code: character = SAVE20 #>   discount: numeric = 0.2 new_Coupon(code = "AB", discount = 0.2) #> Error: #> ! Validation failed for field 'code' in Coupon

title: Validators and models
text: update_<Class>() preferred way mutate — preserves S3 class revalidates: mutation across models without class-specific updater, use update_model().
code: schema_keys <- names(get_schema(p)) schema_keys #> [1] "name"  "age"   "email"  is_model(p) #> [1] TRUE model_to_list(p) #> <Typed Model: Person> #> Fields: #>   name: character = Ada #>   age: integer = 36 #>   email: character = ada@example.com  result <- validate_model(p) result$valid #> [1] TRUE p2 <- update_Person(p, age = 37L) p2$age #> [1] 37

what: What you can do with it
title: Type Safety and Validation for R
text: Check types runtime. is_type(), assert_type(), validate_type(), coerce_type() everyday cases. Wrap functions type checks. typed_function() validates arguments every call return value every exit. Describe data typed models. define_model("Class", fields = list(…)) generates new_Class() update_Class() constructors field-level validation, defaults, nullability. Compose richer types. t_union(), t_nullable(), t_list_of(), t_vector_of(), t_enum(), t_model(), t_predicate() build specs work everywhere type name . Reach ecosystems. Export import JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12), Open Data Contract Standard v3, OpenAPI 3.1 without leaving R. Retrofit whole package. enable_for_package(), as_typed_from_roxygen(), enable_typed_namespace() add type checks across existing package without rewriting functions.

title: Type Safety and Validation for R
code: library(typethis)  # 1. Type checks is_type(1:3, "integer")            # TRUE assert_type(42, "character", "x")  # Error: 'x' must be character, got integer  # 2. Typed functions greet <- typed_function(   function(name, times = 1L) paste(rep(name, times), collapse = " "),   arg_specs = list(name = "character", times = "integer"),   return_spec = "character" ) greet("hi", times = 3L)            # "hi hi hi" greet("hi", times = "3")           # Error: 'times' must be integer, got character  # 3. Typed models define_model("Point", fields = list(   x = field("numeric"),   y = field("numeric") )) p <- new_Point(x = 1, y = 2) update_Point(p, x = 5)$x           # 5  # 4. Composable specs status <- t_enum(c("new", "paid", "shipped")) is_type("paid", status)            # TRUE is_type("done", status)            # FALSE

what: Retrofit type stability onto an existing function — as_typed
title: Retrofit type stability onto an existing function — as_typed
text: Convenience wrapper around typed_function() adding type checks function already . Compared typed_function() removes two sources friction: Argument specs passed via ... instead arg_specs = list(...). Specs arguments literal atomic defaults inferred automatically (see infer_specs()); arguments care need appear .... Internally as_typed() builds arg_specs list delegates typed_function(), calling conventions, coercion, metadata behaviour identical.

title: Retrofit type stability onto an existing function — as_typed
code: as_typed(   fn,   ...,   .return = NULL,   .infer = TRUE,   .validate = TRUE,   .coerce = FALSE )

title: Retrofit type stability onto an existing function — as_typed
text: fn function retrofit. ... Named type specs. Names must match formals fn. Values may character builtins, predicates, type_spec objects. Pass NULL opt single argument inference. .return Specification return value, NULL skip. return type never inferred. .infer TRUE (default), arguments named ... get spec inferred default value possible. See infer_specs(). .validate FALSE, type checks skipped (useful hot paths). .coerce TRUE, arguments match first run coerce_type() assertion.

title: Retrofit type stability onto an existing function — as_typed
text: typed function. shape result typed_function().

title: Retrofit type stability onto an existing function — as_typed
code: # Inferred from defaults — no spec list needed add <- as_typed(function(x = 0L, y = 0L) x + y, .return = "integer") add(2L, 3L) #> [1] 5  # Override one argument; the rest are inferred greet <- as_typed(   function(name = "world", times = 1L) {     paste(rep(name, times), collapse = " ")   },   name = t_vector_of("character", exact_length = 1L) ) greet("hi", times = 3L) #> [1] "hi hi hi"  # Opt an argument out of validation with NULL f <- as_typed(function(x = 1L, y = 1L) x + y, y = NULL) attr(f, "arg_specs") #> $x #> [1] "integer" #>   # Disable inference entirely g <- as_typed(function(x = 1L, y = 2L) x + y, .infer = FALSE) attr(g, "arg_specs") #> list()

what: Bulk-retrofit every function in an environment — as_typed_env
title: Bulk-retrofit every function in an environment — as_typed_env
text: Walks envir, applies as_typed() every function finds, reassigns result back envir place. Useful adding type checks across script, chunk analysis code globalenv(), private environment held package. Per-function overrides flow .specs, named list lists whose entries match as_typed() argument shape (named arg specs plus optional .return/.infer/.validate/.coerce). Functions without .specs entry still retrofitted via inference (.infer = TRUE) function-level defaults. Already-typed functions re-wrapped as_typed()'s idempotent merge path — double-wrapping. Locked bindings (common namespaces) skipped default; single warning reports count. Pass .unlock = TRUE unlock-modify-relock binding place — enable_typed_namespace() uses retrofit bindings namespace locked.

title: Bulk-retrofit every function in an environment — as_typed_env
code: as_typed_env(   envir,   .specs = list(),   .infer = TRUE,   .validate = TRUE,   .coerce = FALSE,   .filter = NULL,   .unlock = FALSE )

title: Bulk-retrofit every function in an environment — as_typed_env
text: envir environment. Use new.env() globalenv() ordinary cases; passing namespace supported bindings locked. .specs Named list per-function override lists. entry shape as_typed()'s ... plus dotted options. Names must correspond functions envir; unknown names error. .infer, .validate, .coerce Function-level defaults forwarded as_typed(). Per-function entries .specs win. .filter Optional function(name, fn) returning single logical; functions returns FALSE skipped. .unlock TRUE, locked bindings temporarily unlocked, reassigned typed wrapper, re-locked. Defaults FALSE, case locked bindings skipped warning. Re-locking guaranteed .exit even wrap step errors.

title: Bulk-retrofit every function in an environment — as_typed_env
text: Invisibly, character vector names retrofitted.

title: Bulk-retrofit every function in an environment — as_typed_env
code: e <- new.env() e$add <- function(x = 0L, y = 0L) x + y e$greet <- function(name = "world") paste0("hi ", name) as_typed_env(e) is_typed(e$add) #> [1] TRUE is_typed(e$greet) #> [1] TRUE  # Per-function overrides e2 <- new.env() e2$add <- function(x = 0L, y = 0L) x + y as_typed_env(e2, .specs = list(   add = list(x = "integer", y = "integer", .return = "integer") ))  # Filter to a subset e3 <- new.env() e3$keep <- function(x = 1L) x e3$skip <- function(x = 1L) x as_typed_env(e3, .filter = function(name, fn) name == "keep")  # Unlock-and-modify locked bindings (e.g. after a namespace lock) e4 <- new.env() e4$add <- function(x = 0L) x lockBinding("add", e4) as_typed_env(e4, .unlock = TRUE) is_typed(e4$add) #> [1] TRUE

what: Retrofit a package using its roxygen / Rd documentation — as_typed_from_roxygen
title: Retrofit a package using its roxygen / Rd documentation — as_typed_from_roxygen
text: Reads installed .Rd documentation pkgname, extracts type spec every documented \\arguments item \\value block, applies result via enable_for_package(). Two extraction layers run sequence: Explicit tag. description begins [type] (e.g. @param x [integer] Number iterations) parsed verbatim. Whatever sits brackets becomes spec string. Vocabulary heuristic. Otherwise description's leading prose matched small vocabulary common R type names (numeric, integer, character, logical, ...). patterns anchored start description, prose like "numeric vector" matches "function applied ..." . See default_type_vocabulary(). resulting .specs list merged anything pass explicitly (overrides win), filtered formals actually exist function, forwarded enable_for_package(). means inference defaults still runs arguments docs describe. Use existing package already decent prose docs type information — typethis lifts already written. specs heuristics recover, add explicit [type] tags roxygen, pass via .specs.

title: Retrofit a package using its roxygen / Rd documentation — as_typed_from_roxygen
code: as_typed_from_roxygen(   pkgname,   .specs = list(),   .infer = TRUE,   .validate = TRUE,   .coerce = FALSE,   .filter = NULL,   .vocabulary = default_type_vocabulary(),   .rd_dir = NULL )

title: Retrofit a package using its roxygen / Rd documentation — as_typed_from_roxygen
text: pkgname Package name (string) installed package, environment together .rd_dir testing / source workflows. .specs, .infer, .validate, .coerce, .filter Forwarded enable_for_package(). Per-function entries .specs win Rd-derived specs. .vocabulary named character vector mapping perl-regex patterns spec strings. first matching pattern wins, order matters. Defaults default_type_vocabulary(). .rd_dir Optional path directory containing .Rd files. supplied, files parsed disk instead via tools::Rd_db(). Useful testing source packages installed.

title: Retrofit a package using its roxygen / Rd documentation — as_typed_from_roxygen
code: # Inside R/zzz.R of your package: # .onLoad <- function(libname, pkgname) { #   typethis::as_typed_from_roxygen(pkgname) # }  # Demonstration with a synthetic Rd directory: rd_dir <- tempfile("rd") dir.create(rd_dir) writeLines(c(   "\\name{add}",   "\\alias{add}",   "\\title{Add two numbers}",   "\\arguments{",   "\\item{x}{A numeric vector.}",   "\\item{y}{[integer] Number of times.}",   "}",   "\\value{A numeric vector.}" ), file.path(rd_dir, "add.Rd"))  ns <- new.env() ns$add <- function(x, y) rep(x, y) as_typed_from_roxygen(ns, .rd_dir = rd_dir) get_signature(ns$add) #> $args #> $args$x #> [1] "numeric" #>  #> $args$y #> [1] "integer" #>  #>  #> $return #> [1] "numeric" #>  #> $formals #> $formals$x #>  #>  #> $formals$y #>  #>  #>

title: Open Data Contract Standard (ODCS v3) bridge — datacontract
text: Convert typed models Open Data Contract Standard (ODCS) v3 YAML format, run datacontract CLI R. Pydantic kind bridge: datacontract export --format pydantic-model generates Pydantic source code contract. typethis closes loop R: typed models can exported ODCS YAML, existing ODCS contracts can loaded back model registry runtime new_*() / update_*() constructors. Constructs without native ODCS representation (data frames, factors, unions, custom predicate functions) emitted x-typethis-* extension keys bridge round-trips typethis-aware tooling. Key entry points: to_datacontract() / write_datacontract() — export. read_datacontract() / from_datacontract() — import. datacontract_lint() / datacontract_test() / datacontract_export() — thin wrappers around upstream CLI.

what: Enable type checking for an entire package — enable_for_package
title: Enable type checking for an entire package — enable_for_package
text: Convenience wrapper switching typethis across every function package namespace, single call package's .onLoad() hook. Inside .onLoad() namespace bindings yet locked, function can replaced place typed wrapper. Add R/zzz.R package containing:   single line walks namespace, infers type specs function's literal atomic defaults (see infer_specs()), replaces every binding typed wrapper. Functions whose defaults inferred left untyped unless add explicit override via .specs. Standard package hook functions (.onLoad, .onAttach, .onUnload, .onDetach, .Last.lib, .First.lib) primitives skipped automatically. user-supplied .filter runs default skip list — can narrow set retrofitted functions, widen . Calling enable_for_package() outside .onLoad() safe usually redundant: namespace locked bindings skipped single warning reporting count.
code: .onLoad <- function(libname, pkgname) {   typethis::enable_for_package(pkgname) }

title: Enable type checking for an entire package — enable_for_package

title: Enable type checking for an entire package — enable_for_package
text: pkgname package name (string), directly environment retrofit. Inside .onLoad() use pkgname argument R passes hook. Passing environment mainly useful tests. .specs, .infer, .validate, .coerce Forwarded as_typed_env(). Per-function entries .specs win inference. .filter Optional function(name, fn) returning single logical. Applied built-skip list, can reduce retrofitted. .unlock TRUE, locked bindings temporarily unlocked re-locked instead skipped. Used enable_typed_namespace() retrofitting namespace locked R.

title: Enable type checking for an entire package — enable_for_package
text: Invisibly, character vector names retrofitted.

title: Enable type checking for an entire package — enable_for_package
code: # Inside R/zzz.R of your own package: # .onLoad <- function(libname, pkgname) { #   typethis::enable_for_package(pkgname) # }  # Demonstration on an ordinary environment shaped like a namespace: ns <- new.env() ns$add <- function(x = 0L, y = 0L) x + y ns$.onLoad <- function(libname, pkgname) NULL enable_for_package(ns) is_typed(ns$add) # TRUE  -- inferred from defaults #> [1] TRUE is_typed(ns$.onLoad) # FALSE -- hook skipped #> [1] FALSE  # Override one function while the rest are inferred ns2 <- new.env() ns2$add <- function(x = 0L, y = 0L) x + y ns2$greet <- function(name = "world") paste0("hi ", name) enable_for_package(ns2, .specs = list(   add = list(.return = "integer") ))

what: Enable type checking for an installed package, without editing it — enable_typed_namespace
title: Enable type checking for an installed package, without editing it — enable_typed_namespace
text: Registers setHook(packageEvent(pkgname, "onLoad"), ...) handler runs enable_for_package() pkgname's namespace time loads, applies retrofit immediately package already loaded. Use — want — add R/zzz.R target package. hook fires R locks namespace bindings, enable_typed_namespace() retrofits binding via unlock-modify-relock dance (see as_typed_env(.unlock = TRUE)). Re-locking guaranteed .exit even wrap step errors. Typical use .Rprofile interactive session:   Standard package hook functions (.onLoad, .onAttach, ...) primitives skipped automatically enable_for_package(). pattern CRAN-bound code: modifying another package's namespace outside developer convenience, shipping feature. Use enable_for_package() package's .onLoad production code.

title: Composable type specifications — type_spec
text: Structured, composable type specifications. work everywhere type name — is_type(), assert_type(), validate_type(), field(), typed_function(), to_json_schema() — compose: t_list_of(t_union("integer", "character")) valid.

title: Composable type specifications — type_spec
text: constructors return objects class type_spec. Plain character strings (e.g. "numeric") predicate functions continue work type arguments without change; internally normalized type_spec objects. Available constructors: t_union() — match several alternatives. t_nullable() — also accept NULL. t_list_of() — list elements given type, optional length. t_vector_of() — atomic vector given builtin type, length. t_enum() — value fixed set. t_model() — instance registered typed model. t_predicate() — wrap predicate function description. Use is_type_spec() detect composite spec runtime.

what: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
title: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
text: Returns wrapped version fn validates argument spec arg_specs every call, (optionally) return value return_spec. Calls violate spec raise informative error — just — fn runs. Argument specs may character builtins, predicate functions, type_spec (e.g. t_union(), t_list_of()). Coercion can enabled per call via coerce = TRUE. R calling conventions supported: positional, named, reordered named, mixed, ... passthrough.

title: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function

title: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
text: fn underlying function. arg_specs Named list (character vector) argument specifications. Names must match argument names fn. return_spec Specification return value, NULL skip. validate FALSE, type checks skipped (useful hot paths). coerce TRUE, arguments match first run coerce_type() assertion. arg_types, return_type Deprecated aliases arg_specs / return_spec. New code use latter.

title: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
text: function formals fn. Carries arg_specs, return_spec, typed = TRUE attributes.

title: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
code: add <- typed_function(   function(x, y) x + y,   arg_specs = c(x = "numeric", y = "numeric"),   return_spec = "numeric" ) add(2, 3) #> [1] 5 add(x = 2, y = 3) #> [1] 5 add(y = 3, x = 2) #> [1] 5  # Argument violation err <- tryCatch(add("a", "b"), error = function(e) conditionMessage(e)) err #> [1] "Type error: 'x' must be numeric, got character"  # ... passthrough total <- typed_function(   function(x, ...) sum(x, ...),   arg_specs = c(x = "numeric") ) total(c(1, NA, 3), na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 4  # Coercion add_lenient <- typed_function(   function(x, y) x + y,   arg_specs = c(x = "numeric", y = "numeric"),   coerce = TRUE ) add_lenient("5", "3") #> [1] 8

what: Get or set the type specs of a function — types
title: Get or set the type specs of a function — types
text: Symmetric replacement-form accessor as_typed(). setter delegates as_typed(); getter returns specs shape setter accepts, types(f) <- types(g) round-trips. types(f) returns list whose named entries argument specs whose .return entry (present) return spec. Returns list() untyped function, cheap use (length(types(f))) ... checks. types(f) <- value retrofits f via as_typed(). value must list — named arg specs plus .return, .infer, .validate, .coerce. types(f) <- NULL un-types f (returns original inner function) — natural inverse.

what: typethis: runtime type safety and validation for R — typethis-package
title: typethis: runtime type safety and validation for R — typethis-package
text: typethis brings type checks, validators, typed data models R. Errors surface originate, messages name offending field argument expected.

title: typethis: runtime type safety and validation for R — typethis-package
text: exported API grouped following families. help page function lists family "See also" section, know one function can navigate neighbours. Type checking is_type(), assert_type(), validate_type(), is_one_of(), coerce_type() — core runtime checks. Type specifications t_union(), t_nullable(), t_list_of(), t_vector_of(), t_enum(), t_model(), t_predicate(), is_type_spec() — composable specs work everywhere type name . Validators numeric_range(), string_length(), string_pattern(), vector_length(), dataframe_spec(), enum_validator(), list_of(), nullable(), combine_validators(), validator_constraint() — value-level rules attached fields arguments. Typed functions typed_function(), signature(), with_signature(), is_typed(), get_signature(), typed_method(), validate_call() — wrap function call validated. Typed models define_model(), field(), is_model(), get_schema(), validate_model(), update_model(), model_to_list() — describe record type field-level validation, defaults, nullability. JSON Schema export to_json_schema() — emit JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) fragments typed models, type specs, validators. Data Contract bridge to_datacontract(), write_datacontract(), read_datacontract(), from_datacontract(), datacontract_lint(), datacontract_test(), datacontract_export(), datacontract_cli_available() — round-trip Open Data Contract Standard v3. title: typethis (development version)
text: enable_for_package() runs as_typed() every exported function installed package’s namespace one call. Per-function specs flow .specs = list(funA = list(...)); optional .filter predicate narrows set functions touched. Already-typed functions go as_typed()’s idempotent merge path.

title: typethis (development version)
text: as_typed_from_roxygen() reads installed .Rd files (source man/ directory via .rd_dir), extracts type spec documented \arguments item \value block, forwards result via enable_for_package(). Two extraction layers run sequence: explicit [type] prefix start description, vocabulary heuristic default_type_vocabulary() maps leading prose like “numeric vector” “TRUE” corresponding builtin spec. Rd-derived specs pruned formals actually exist function; user-supplied .specs win conflict. parse_param_type() exported users can preview derived single description string. default_type_vocabulary() exported users can extend replace prose--spec map.

title: typethis (development version)
text: enable_typed_namespace() registers setHook(packageEvent(pkg, "onLoad"), ...) handler runs enable_for_package() namespace time loads. package already loaded called, retrofit also applies immediately live namespace. hook fires R locks namespace bindings, retrofit goes unlock-modify-relock dance, re-locking guaranteed .exit. disable_typed_namespace() inverse: removes typethis-tagged hooks package event (foreign hooks left intact) , default, walks loaded namespace revert typed wrapper inner function. as_typed_env() gained .unlock parameter. TRUE, locked bindings unlocked, reassigned typed wrapper, re-locked instead skipped + warned. enable_for_package() forwards .unlock . pattern meant development exploratory use, CRAN-bound code: modifying namespaces developer convenience. See vignette("package-wide") full walkthrough.

title: typethis (development version)
text: as_typed_env() retrofits every function environment one call. walks environment, applies as_typed() function (inference default), writes typed versions back place. Per-function overrides flow .specs = list(funA = list(...)); optional .filter predicate narrows set functions touched. Already-typed functions go as_typed()’s existing idempotent merge path, locked bindings (common namespaces) skipped single warning. types(f) / types(f) <- value symmetric replacement-form accessor as_typed(). getter returns list whose named entries arg specs whose .return entry (present) return spec — shape setter accepts, types(g) <- types(f) round-trips. types(f) <- NULL un-types f returns original inner function. two follow-ups deferred v0.6 NEWS entry.

title: typethis (development version)
text: as_typed() convenience wrapper around typed_function() adding type checks function already . Argument specs passed via ... instead arg_specs = list(...), specs arguments literal atomic defaults (1L, 1.5, "", TRUE) inferred automatically. Pass name = NULL opt single argument , .infer = FALSE disable inference entirely. as_typed() idempotent — re-wrapping typed function merges new specs existing wrapper instead stacking another layer. infer_specs() returns inferred argument specs function named list, useful inspection building spec manually. Possible follow-ups deferred later release: bulk as_typed_env() helper retrofitting whole namespaces, replacement-style types(f) <- form.

title: typethis (development version)
text: to_openapi() (S3 generic) converts typed models, model constructors, instances, typed functions, mixed lists OpenAPI 3.1 document fragment. Schemas land components.schemas; $ref strings rewritten JSON Schema’s #/$defs/X form OpenAPI’s #/components/schemas/X form. Typed functions become paths entry JSON requestBody (arg_specs entry property; arguments without defaults end required) 200 response derived return_spec. from_openapi() reads OpenAPI 3.x document (path, URL, parsed list) registers entry components.schemas typed model generated new_*() / update_*() constructors. $refs components.schemas resolve t_model() references; inline object properties registered typed models. write_openapi() / read_openapi() wrap file IO. Format inferred file extension (.yaml/.yml → YAML via yaml, .json → JSON via jsonlite); pass format = "yaml" / "json" override. bridge sits top to_json_schema() (v0.3) — composite type specs validator constraints flow unchanged, new mapping code needed.

title: typethis (development version)
text: jsonlite now also used (addition JSON Schema export) write_openapi(format = "json") / read_openapi() .json paths. Still Suggests.

title: typethis (development version)
text: coerce_type() now accepts type_spec argument composable cases coercion clear meaning: t_nullable() (NULL passes , otherwise inner spec drives coercion), t_union() (alternative tried order; first coerces validates wins), t_enum() (value accepted directly already allowed set, coerced enum’s value type re-checked). type_spec kinds (t_list_of(), t_vector_of(), t_model(), t_predicate()) still error, clearer message naming unsupported kind. README vignette updated cover v0.3 (composable type specs, JSON Schema export) v0.4 (Data Contract bridge).

title: typethis (development version)
text: New test nested-object registration from_datacontract() (covers implicit define_model_in() side-effect nested ODCS object properties). New .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml runs R CMD check (errors warnings) macOS, Windows, Ubuntu (release, devel, oldrel-1) plus lintr job every push PR.

title: typethis (development version)
text: to_datacontract() (S3 generic) converts typed models, model constructors, instances Open Data Contract Standard v3.x contracts native R lists, ready yaml::write_yaml(). write_datacontract() writes contract directly .yaml file. from_datacontract() reads contract YAML (path, URL, parsed list) registers schema entry typed model generated new_*() / update_*() constructors. read_datacontract() parses contract YAML without registering anything. Thin wrappers around datacontract CLI: datacontract_lint(), datacontract_test(), datacontract_export(), plus datacontract_cli_available() capability detection. field() gained ODCS metadata arguments: primary_key, unique, pii, classification, tags, examples, references, quality. round-trip contract bridge introspectable, effect runtime validation. Validator constraints (numeric_range, string_length, string_pattern, vector_length, enum_validator, dataframe_spec) emitted native ODCS constraint fields (minimum, maxLength, pattern, enum, …). Constructs without native ODCS mapping (data frames, factors, unions, custom predicate functions) emit x-typethis-* extension keys bridge round-trips typethis-aware tooling.

title: typethis (development version)
text: yaml (>= 2.3.0) added Suggests. Required write_datacontract(), read_datacontract(), from_datacontract().

title: typethis (development version)
text: New type_spec S3 class structured, composable type specifications. New constructors: t_union(), t_nullable(), t_list_of(), t_vector_of(), t_enum(), t_model(), t_predicate(). compose: t_list_of(t_union("integer", "character")) valid. composite specs work is_type(), assert_type(), typed_function() (arg_specs/return_spec), field(). Backward compatible: plain character builtin names predicate functions continue work everywhere . Helper is_type_spec() detect composite specs.

title: typethis (development version)
text: New to_json_schema() (S3 generic) serializes typed models, type specs, validator closures, field() definitions JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) fragments suitable jsonlite::toJSON(). Methods: to_json_schema.default(), to_json_schema.type_spec(), to_json_schema.typed_model(). Builtin validator factories (numeric_range, string_length, string_pattern, vector_length, enum_validator, list_of, dataframe_spec, nullable, combine_validators) now attach structured constraint attribute returned closure. Use validator_constraint() read . closure otherwise unchanged remains callable . Nested models become $ref entries auto-populated $defs; cyclic references terminate via stub--fill protocol. Constructs without canonical JSON Schema mapping (data frames, factors, custom predicates) emit x-typethis-* extension keys.
